steps.title: Match the two triangles
steps.content: Because $AB=AC$, the base angles of triangle $ABC$ are congruent. The problem also gives angles $BED$ and $CFD$ congruent, so triangles $BED$ and $CFD$ are similar.

steps.title: Use the side ratio
steps.content:
Point $D$ makes 
$$
BD=\frac{5}{8}BC
$$, so 
$$
DC=\frac{3}{8}BC
$$ Therefore,
$$\frac{BD}{DC}=\frac{5}{3}$$
The corresponding side ratio gives
$$\frac{BE}{CF}=\frac{5}{3}$$

steps.title: Solve the missing length
steps.content: Substitute $BE=15$ and solve. $$\frac{15}{CF}=\frac{5}{3}$$ $$5CF=45$$ $$CF=9$$ The answer is 9.
